title: snATAC-Seq on 10x ChromiumTM platform for fresh, frozen and cryopreserved material: my notes from the lab (UPDATED VERSION)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2o2d2","text":"-Here, I will only include tips and notes related to snATAC workflow. guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e96jn","text":"Protocol Overview","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a922q","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"ff0in","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"evnjg","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"50vmm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"47hog","text":"Use a plastic pestle to mechanically homogenize tissue and release nuclei","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"10k2t","text":"Separate the nuclei from debris using a cell sorter (if not, then see note at the end)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f247n","text":"Collect a specific number of nuclei in a 96-well plate containing 10x RT Buffer* or Wash Buffer**","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2m1ih","text":"Immediately load the sample into a Single Cell Chip for processing according the Single Cell 3’ v3 Reagents User Guide or Single Cell V(D)J 5’ Reagents User Guide.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eujuv","text":"In our hands the use of DAPI, 7-AAD and DRAQ-7 dyes show very little or no effect on ATAC data metrics. ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5bj2t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"avgag","text":"*Consider the event overestimation of some sorters (Check step 19). Also, assume that nuclei recovery is ~ 57 %; use this to determine the number of nuclei to collect for each of your samples. [This value is derived from the Cell Suspension Volume Calculator Table in the Single Cell 3’ and v3 - Reagents User Guide or Single Cell V(D)J 5’ v1 Reagents User Guide]","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c901f","text":"**Assume that nuclei loss can be up to ~ 50 — 60 % due to loss during washing and counting; use this to determine the number of nuclei to collect for each of your This will ensure you avoid over- or under-lysis the cells. ","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"fp7lq","text":"","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"length":1,"offset":67}],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"ne2i","text":"-The cell lysis and washes are always carried out on wet-ice (i.e.   ) and in the presence of RNAse inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL.","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"46kj1","text":"","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[{"key":5,"length":1,"offset":44}],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"nci4","text":"-Centrifugation, in my hands, works well at   and this is enough for most of the tissues types I worked on. NOTE:","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"epk1n","text":"","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":7,"offset":15,"style":"UNDERLINE"}],"key":"hue9","text":"If you are NOT sorting nuclei that is in Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer directly into ATAC Wash Buffer-Dig, avoid resuspending pelleted nuclei that was in Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer (PBS-based) directly in Diluted Nuclei Buffer or ATAC Wash Buffer-Dig as nuclei tend to clump.","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"198va","text":"","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":16,"offset":26,"style":"italic"},{"length":2,"offset":160,"style":"sub"},{"length":14,"offset":231,"style":"bold"}],"key":"32ejt","text":"Instead, you need to do a buffer exchange (from PBS to Tris) by adding at least 2x the volume of ATAC Wash Buffer-Dig [Tris-HCl 10 mM (pH 7.4), NaCl 10 mM, MgCl2 3 mM, BSA 1%, Tween-20 0.1 %, Digitonin 0.01%] to the nuclei that is in suspension in the Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer (PBS-based).","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"fcsvd","text":"","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"length":1,"offset":20}],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"7d79j","text":"Let steps: Ideally, perform the following steps WHILE the RT reaction is RUNNING. See Frankenstein protocol for nuclei prep. The steps below assumes the nuclei input for snATAC is relatively low. Sort as many nuclei as possible into a round-bottom 96-well plate well containing 100 µL of ice-cold ATAC Wash Buffer-Dig. DO NOT, I repeat DO NOT sort into Diluted Nuclei Buffer. Avoid excessive number of steps to reduce nuclei loss. Work as quicky as you can. Always leave some volume (10-15 uL) of supernatant behind when washing. Note there will be significant nuclei loss during washes and nuclei counting, so you may want to make sure the washing steps are done carefully. Take into account this loss when deciding aimed nuclei. To reduce loss, follow the tips below ( bold ).
Transfer the entire volume to 0.2 ml PCR tube (LoBind!).
Add 50 µL of ATAC Wash Buffer-Dig to the well.
Transfer any remanent nuclei to the 0.2 ml PCR tube (~ 150 µL ).
Centrifuge the nuclei at 500 x g for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .
Gently add 100 µL ice-cold Diluted Nuclei Buffer. DO NOT resuspend nuclei. This is just a buffer exchange step.
Centrifuge the nuclei at 500 x g for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .
Remove 100 µL of the supernatant in two steps , namely, remove 90 µL first and then 10 µL (pellet may not be visible!).
Gently add 100 µL ice-cold Diluted Nuclei Buffer. DO NOT resuspend nuclei. This is just a buffer exchange step.
Centrifuge the nuclei at 500 x g for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .

Remove the supernatant (~ 100 µL ) in two steps , namely, remove 90 µL first and then as much volume as possible to leave leaving behind ~ 7 µL — 10 µL (avoid disturbing pellet) . If you manage to remove the whole volume without disturbing the pellet, then add 7 µL of Diluted Nuclei Buffer and proceed to step 13.
Resuspend nuclei in the ~ 7 µL — 10 µL of ice-cold Diluted Nuclei Buffer, carefully washing walls of the tube to ensure all nuclei are in solution.
Take 1 µL — 2 µL and dilute 1:5 with Diluted Nuclei Buffer.

Mix 1:1 with Trypan Blue and count the number of nuclei with a cell counter (Countess II FL Automated Cell Counter) or hematocytometer (the counting is to have an idea of how many nuclei to expect based on the recovery factor). Note that Trypan Blue (TB) tend to precipitate, so centrifuge vial of TP at top speed and use supernatant for staining.
Inspect under the microscope. Nuclei should look clean and intact. See attached photo: Fig. 4: Representative photographs of nuclei post sorting and clean up, right before entering 10x ATAC workflow.

Take 5 µL of nuclei in Diluted Nuclei Buffer and proceed directly to Chromium Single Cell ATAC Reagent Kits protocol (CG000168 Rev A). The volume added to the Transposition reaction will vary; for low input samples we usually use all 5 µL of nuclei prep. Alternatively, follow recommendations of the User Guide to estimate volume of nuclei to add to recover a determined targeted nuclei recovery (Page 20, CG000168 Rev A). To estimate the Number of Recovered Nuclei, do the following calculation: [Nuclei Concentration (from step 35) x Volume of Nuclei (up to 5 μl)] / 1.53 (recovery efficiency factor)
creator: Luciano G Martelotto
